rulings.Case Title: ROGER HENDERSON VS. CROWN CORK & SEAL COMPANY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order To Consolidate Actions For All Purposes Including Trial
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 9, 2014 in Department 503, Line 1. Plaintiffs' motion to consolidate actions for all purposes including trial is granted. No opposition filed. Cases numbered 275781 and 276272 are consolidated. Henceforth all papers shall be filed under the lead case no. 275781. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JAY B. BARNES et al VS. 3M COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Alternatively, Summary Adjudication Of Issues
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 9, 2014 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant Arvinmeritor, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment or, alternatively, summary adjudication is off calendar at the moving party's request. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS LEO DOUGLAS TREMBLAY VS. ADERHOLT SPECIALTY COMPANY INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION As To Deft San Francisco Gravel Co., Inc.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 9, 2014 in Department 503, Line 3. Plaintiffs' motion for summary adjudication as to defendant San Francisco Gravel Company, Inc.: hearing required. The hearing will be at 9:45a.m. Both sides must bring proposed orders to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES SHALL et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Owens-Illinois, Inc.S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Or Stay For Forum Non Conveniens
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, October 9, 2014 in Department 503, Line 4. Defendant Owens-Illinois, Inc.'s motion to dismiss on the grounds of forum non conveniens is continued to October 30, 2014 per the October 8, 2014 stipulation and order. = (503/TLJ)
